questions.text: You are preparing for Google Professional Data Engineer and reviewing Designing data processing systems. Which source aligns with official Google Cloud documentation and exam guides?
questions.explanation: Use PDE exam guide and other official materials listed in the exam guide—not copied exam items.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to batch and stream with Apache Beam. Which solution is BEST?
While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must orchestrate batch pipelines. Which action is appropriate?
Which Google Cloud service should you use to batch and stream with Apache Beam?
What is the recommended Google Cloud solution to batch and stream with Apache Beam?
A platform team must batch and stream with Apache Beam. Which option aligns with Google best practices?
Which Google Cloud service should you use to orchestrate batch pipelines?
What is the recommended Google Cloud solution to orchestrate batch pipelines?
A platform team must orchestrate batch pipelines. Which option aligns with Google best practices?
For cost-aware operations, you should batch and stream with Apache Beam.
A data team must orchestrate batch pipelines.
Your startup is building on Google Cloud and needs to batch and stream with Apache Beam.
A cloud engineer needs to orchestrate batch pipelines.

Two designs were proposed. Design A would batch and stream with Apache Beam. Design B would orchestrate batch pipelines. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Dataflow runs Beam pipelines serverlessly. Compare with Cloud Composer (managed Airflow): Composer schedules complex workflows.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would orchestrate batch pipelines. Design B would batch and stream with Apache Beam. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Composer schedules complex workflows. Compare with Cloud Dataflow: Dataflow runs Beam pipelines serverlessly.
